title: Tensor rifle
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}}
{{Update|[[Lone Wolf: A Tale of Obi-Wan and Luke|Star Wars: Lone Wolf: A Tale of Obi-Wan and Luke]]}}

[[Amberlandrax Armaments]]' '''tensor rifle''', often referred to as the '''Squib tensor rifle''', was a [[Tensor weapon|weapon]] of [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] design that used [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] technology to generate damaging waves of tensor energy. The [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]] fired an invisible yet noisy burst of compressed tractor beam waves; the constant barrage affected a biological target at the cellular level and eventually resulted in [[Death/Legends|death]]. Variants of the tensor weapon used either [[Power generator/Legends|power generators]] or standard [[Power cell/Legends|power packs]] as an energy source. The device was small enough to fit in Squib [[Limb/Legends|hands]], although it was difficult to aim and fire correctly without specialized skill.

The tensor rifle was available for sale in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Although retailers such as [[Galladinium Galactic Exports]] offered the weapon in their [[Datalog/Legends|datalog]], use of the tensor rifle was strictly regulated and even illegal in most jurisdictions. The weapon's rarity also curtailed its widespread adoption.

==Description==
[[File:Annoying_Squib.jpg|thumb|left|upright|The tensor rifle was of Squib design.]]
[[Amberlandrax Armaments]]' tensor [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]] was designed with a handle and trigger for one of the user's [[Limb/Legends|hands]], with a foregrip for the other hand and a flared muzzle at the fore. The weapon drew upon [[Squib/Legends|Squib]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]] and manufacturing to harness the [[Species/Legends|species]]' mastery of [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] technology. The resulting rifle was part of a general class known as [[tensor weapon]]s, which emitted quick pulses of compressed tractor beam waves to pummel a target with a constant barrage of cell-destroying<ref name="GFT 89" /> tensor energy from a distance.<ref name="UR 38" /> Upon hitting a mark, the rifle's wielder was able to focus the beam on the adversary; the injury caused increased with [[Time/Legends|time]] until the victim finally [[Death/Legends|died]]. Nevertheless, Amberlandrax's rifle shot a beam that was invisible to the naked [[Eye/Legends|eye]], a design that made it difficult to aim accurately. The weapon had a maximum range of 250 [[Meter/Legends|meters]],<ref name="GFT 89" /> but the unwieldy beam made rapid firing impossible.<ref name="A&EG" /> The gun created a din so loud that Squibs who owned the weapon learned to use it to get the attention of beings who might otherwise dismiss them.<ref name="UR 38" />

The tensor rifle was small enough to be wielded by a single combatant,<ref name="GFT 89" /> even one the size of a typical one-[[Meter/Legends|meter]], twenty-two-[[Kilogram/Legends|kilogram]] Squib. The weapon itself weighed 7.2 kilograms. Non-Squibs found the tensor rifle difficult to use without specialized training, but Squibs took to it naturally. At least two variants of the weapon were available: one drew energy from conventional [[Power cell/Legends|power packs]] of the type used to power other energy weapons and squeezed out fifteen shots before recharge.<ref name="UR 38" /> Another model instead used a [[Power generator/Legends|power generator]] for energy and provided fifty shots without power replacement.<ref name="GFT 89" />

==History==
[[File:Galladinium_Galactic_Exports.svg|thumb|right|Galladinium Galactic Exports sold the tensor rifle through its datalog.]]
Amberlandrax Armaments manufactured the Squib tensor rifle during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], as early as the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].<ref name="date">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'' notes that the book is set at some point between the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], events that took place in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> The legality of the weapon varied from one area of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to another; the rifle was generally rare except among the Squibs, and its use often required a government-issued license,<ref name="A&EG" /> for which a fee had to be [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|paid]]. Ownership or use of the weapon was completely illegal on many [[Planet/Legends|planets]].<ref name="GFT 89" />

At some point before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="date" /> [[Galladinium Galactic Exports]] nevertheless featured the tensor rifle in the fiftieth-anniversary issue of its [[Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology|''Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology'']], albeit in a special edition of the [[Datalog/Legends|publication]] distributed to those whom the company deemed its "[[Galladinium Preferred Customers|preferred customers]]." As with all its merchandise, Galladinium accepted orders for the tensor rifle via [[Subspace transceiver/Legends|subspace transceivers]], payable by [[credit account]], with extra fees for shipping and handling. The retailer provided a floor model of the tensor rifle for testing at its [[Galladinium Galactic Exports showroom|showroom]] in [[Hreas Port City]], on the [[Basin]] continent of the planet [[Lenthalis]], located in the [[Secossi system]].<ref name="GFT 89" /> The power-pack-ready tensor rifle was available for as little as 10,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]],<ref name="UR 38" /> although Galladinium sold the higher-capacity, power-generator version for 15,000.<ref name="GFT 89" /> The weapon was still available in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] after the transition from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]<ref name="A&EG" /> following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The tensor rifle was designed as part of a contest run by the [[RPGA|Role Playing Game Association Network]] (RPGA) that allowed gamers to submit weapon and equipment designs to [[West End Games]], publisher of ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Although the entry did not win a prize in the competition, it was selected for publication in the [[1995]] sourcebook ''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', compiled by [[Rick D. Stuart]].<ref name="GFT 89" /> The weapon was later adapted for [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying system]], where it was featured in the ''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]'' in [[2002]]<ref name="A&EG" /> and ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' in [[2010]].<ref name="UR 38" /> Both West End's and Wizards' game statistics represent the weapon as dealing damage comparable to a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], and both games require users to learn a unique skill to fire the weapon effectively.<ref name="GFT 89" /><ref name="A&EG">''[[Arms & Equipment Guide]]''</ref>
